problem_text: Determine whether integral_1^infinity 1/x^2 dx converges, and if so find its value.

content.label: Improper: infinite upper limit
content.applies_when: the upper limit is infinite, so the integral is defined as a limit of proper integrals.

content.meaning: integral_a^infinity f dx = lim_{t->infinity} integral_a^t f dx.

content.label: rewrite integral_1^infinity x^-2 dx = lim_{t->infinity} integral_1^t x^-2 dx
content.action: rewrite integral_1^infinity x^-2 dx = lim_{t->infinity} integral_1^t x^-2 dx
content.purpose: convert the improper integral into a limit of proper integrals

content.label: the antiderivative of x^-2 is -1/x, so integral_1^t = [-1/x]_1^t = 1 - 1/t
content.action: the antiderivative of x^-2 is -1/x, so integral_1^t = [-1/x]_1^t = 1 - 1/t
content.purpose: evaluate the proper integral up to the cutoff t

content.label: let t -> infinity: 1/t -> 0, so the limit is 1
content.action: let t -> infinity: 1/t -> 0, so the limit is 1
content.purpose: resolve the limit to find the value of the improper integral

content.label: p-integral at infinity converges for p>1
content.applies_when: the tail integral of x^-p on [1,infinity) has p=2 > 1
